name: Find billing address (postcode lookup and select)
pageTitle: Find an address - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: Billing
heading: Find an address
bodyCopy: You must use a UK address.

Help for non-UK businesses (Details summary) — expands to: If you are a user from the Republic of Ireland and do not have a UK billing address email CUCsupport@defra.gov.uk. This email address will not reply to any other type of enquiry. If you are based elsewhere, you must employ a UK-based import agent to complete your notifications. Import agents must use their own address for non-UK customers and are liable for any charges.

structure.govukComponent: Fieldset legend as page heading (govuk-fieldset__legend--xl, govuk-fieldset__heading)

structure.text: You must use a UK address.
If you are a user from the Republic of Ireland and do not have a UK billing address email CUCsupport@defra.gov.uk.
This email address will not reply to any other type of enquiry.
If you are based elsewhere, you must employ a UK-based import agent to complete your notifications.
Import agents must use their own address for non-UK customers and are liable for any charges.

structure.govukComponent: Label as page heading inside h1 (govuk-label--xl, govuk-label-wrapper)
Body — echoes searched postcode with a 'Change' link back to find-address

structure.text: Select the address — first option 'NN addresses found', then NN address options
structure.govukComponent: Select (govuk-select govuk-!-width-full, plus custom class select-the-address)

structure.text: I cannot find the address in the list
structure.govukComponent: Link (in paragraph; routes to manual address entry /billing-details/address)

govukComponents.usage: "Billing" section caption above the H1 on both the find-address and select-address pages
Wraps the postcode field; the legend doubles as the page H1 'Find an address'
Collapsible 'Help for non-UK businesses' guidance about UK-address requirement and import agents
Postcode label (govuk-label--s) on find-address; select label as H1 (govuk-label--xl) on select-address

Two elements carry govuk-hint on the find-address page: (1) an outer wrapper div (id=find-an-address-hint) that itself contains a govuk-body paragraph 'You must use a UK address.' plus the 'Help for non-UK businesses' Details — note the UK-address line renders as body, not hint text; (2) the postcode field hint 'For example, AA3 1AB' (id=billing-address-postcode-hint). Only the second is a conventional field hint.
govukComponents.evidence:
trace 566993a8e644d92e502bbb170ae3a5122bf5053f, action 102 (div.govuk-hint#find-an-address-hint wraps

You must use a UK address.

+
; separate div.govuk-hint#billing-address-postcode-hint = 'For example, AA3 1AB')

govukComponents.usage: 'Find address' primary button on find-address page; 'Continue' primary button on select-address page
'Select the address' dropdown populated from the postcode lookup (select-address page)

'Enter address manually', 'Cancel and return to notification' (find-address); 'Change' postcode, 'I cannot find the address in the list', 'Cancel and return to notification' (select-address); mailto CUCsupport@defra.gov.uk in Details. All six are plain govuk-link EXCEPT the 'Change' link, which alone adds the govuk-!-font-weight-regular modifier.
govukComponents.evidence: trace 566993a8e644d92e502bbb170ae3a5122bf5053f, action 102 (3 plain govuk-link) + action 104 (Change = 'govuk-link govuk-!-font-weight-regular'; other 2 plain govuk-link)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: select-the-address (extra class on the govuk-select element)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: IPAFFS-specific hook class applied alongside govuk-select on the address dropdown; likely a JS/CSS selector target, not a visual variant.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Non-govuk class, but purely a marker — the control is a standard GOV.UK Select. No structural deviation.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Standard GOV.UK Select (already in use); the hook class can be dropped or replaced with a data-* attribute in the rebuild.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 566993a8e644d92e502bbb170ae3a5122bf5053f, action 104 (select id=addressListBox class="govuk-select govuk-!-width-full select-the-address")

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Postcode-lookup two-step pattern (find-address -> select-address) implemented as a native govuk-select of raw address strings
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Server-side postcode lookup returns N addresses rendered as <option> lines in a plain select; the first option is a non-selectable count placeholder ('46 addresses found').
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not a GOV.UK Design System pattern per se (DS has no address-lookup component), but it stays inside the toolbox by using a plain Select rather than a JS autocomplete widget. The 'NN addresses found' count as option[0] is a bespoke convention. No accessible-autocomplete on this page (unlike some IPAFFS pages).
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Keep the Select-based approach, or use the cross-government 'Find address' pattern; either is achievable with govuk-frontend components only.

fields.name: postalCode
fields.label: Postcode
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — Joi schema on the find-address POST marks postalCode `.required()` with a UK-postcode `.pattern(POSTCODE_REGEX)` (find_an_address_validator.js:15-19). The rendered DOM carried no HTML `required` attribute (confirmed, action 102) because validation is server-side; the field is genuinely mandatory to progress. Rebuild may revisit whether to surface an HTML `required`/`pattern` attribute, but the mandatoriness itself is real.
fields.conditionalOn: CUC (Common User Charge) billing flow — only entered when isCuc=true; reached by clicking the 'Change' address link on the Confirm billing details page (ched-d-workflows.ts:356 confirmBillingDetails.linkChangeAddress.click). isCuc defaults to false (ched-d-workflows.ts:143), so this whole page is a variant-only branch.
fields.evidence: trace 566993a8e644d92e502bbb170ae3a5122bf5053f, action 102 (fill 'B693NE' into getByRole('textbox',{name:'Postcode'}); input id=postalCode name=postalCode, no required attribute observed). Corroborated by page object FindAnAddressPage.ts:8 (inputPostalCode = getByRole('textbox',{name:'Postcode'})) and FindAnAddressPage.ts:16 (searchPostcodeAndContinue fills then clicks 'Find address'); default value 'B693NE' from ched-d-workflows.ts DEFAULT_CONFIG line 144. Tests only ever fill a valid postcode — no empty/invalid error path is exercised. Legacy validator (find_an_address_validator.js:15-19, ipaffs-frontend-notification) confirms the field is `.required()` and pattern-checked server-side.

fields.name: addressListBox
fields.label: Select the address
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — Joi schema on the select-address POST marks addressListBox `.required()` (select_billing_address_validation.js:15-19). The rendered DOM carried no HTML `required` attribute on the select (confirmed, action 104) because validation is server-side; the field is genuinely mandatory to progress. option[0] is the non-selectable count placeholder with value='' (selectBillingAddress.html:42), so leaving it on the placeholder trips the `.required()` empty-string rule → 'Select address from the list'.

fields.conditionalOn: Shown on the /billing-details/select-address page after a successful postcode lookup
fields.evidence: trace 566993a8e644d92e502bbb170ae3a5122bf5053f, action 104 (select id=addressListBox; 47 options = 1 count placeholder value='' + 46 addresses value 0..45; reference/lookup data sourced from a postcode address service, not hardcoded). Corroborated by page object BillingSelectTheAddressPage.ts:7 (dropdownAddressList = getByRole('combobox',{name:'Select the address'}) — confirms the accessible name/label 'Select the address') and BillingSelectTheAddressPage.ts:15 (selectAddressAndContinue selects by option index then clicks 'Continue'); default index '2' from ched-d-workflows.ts DEFAULT_CONFIG line 145, matching the observed selection. Tests select by numeric index into the live lookup list, so no fixed option set is asserted.

validationMessages.message: Enter postcode or enter address manually
validationMessages.trigger: Submitting 'Find address' with the postcode field empty (Joi STRING_EMPTY / ANY_REQUIRED on the required postalCode).
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification validation_messages.js:40 (ENTER_POSTCODE_OR_MANUAL_ERROR_MESSAGE = 'Enter postcode or enter address manually'); wired at find_an_address_validator.js:17-18 (STRING_EMPTY + ANY_REQUIRED → this message). Copy differs from the select-address page's plain 'Enter postcode' because the find-address page offers the 'Enter address manually' escape hatch.

validationMessages.trigger: Submitting 'Find address' with a postcode that fails POSTCODE_REGEX (Joi STRING_PATTERN).
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification validation_messages.js:39 (UK_POSTCODE_ERROR_MESSAGE = 'Enter valid UK postcode'); wired at find_an_address_validator.js:16 (STRING_PATTERN → this message). POSTCODE_REGEX = /^([A-Za-z][A-Ha-hJ-Yj-y]?\d[A-Za-z0-9]? ?\d[A-Za-z]{2}|[Gg][Ii][Rr] ?0[Aa]{2})$/ (regexes.js:13) — standard UK postcode incl. GIR 0AA.

validationMessages.message: No address found. Try again or enter address manually
validationMessages.trigger: A syntactically valid postcode was submitted but the postcode-lookup service returned zero addresses.
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification validation_messages.js:41 (NO_ADDRESS_FOUND_ERROR_MESSAGE = 'No address found. Try again or enter address manually'); applied in the handler at find_an_address.js:13-14,37-41 (when commonUserChargeHelper.doAddressesExistInLookupService returns truthy, sets error.description = NO_ADDRESS_FOUND_ERROR_MESSAGE against POSTCODE_KEY and re-renders). This is a service-result error, not a schema error — no trace exercised it.

validationMessages.trigger: Clicking 'Continue' on the select-address page while the dropdown is left on the non-selectable count placeholder (value='', Joi STRING_EMPTY / ANY_REQUIRED on the required addressListBox).
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification validation_messages.js:50 (SELECT_ADDRESS_ERROR_MESSAGE = 'Select address from the list'); wired at select_billing_address_validation.js:15-19 (STRING_EMPTY + ANY_REQUIRED → this message). The placeholder option carries value='' (selectBillingAddress.html:42), so no explicit selection fails the required check.

validationMessages.trigger: Continue submitted with the hidden postalCode field empty/absent (Joi STRING_EMPTY / ANY_REQUIRED on the required hidden postalCode).
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification validation_messages.js:38 (POSTCODE_ERROR_MESSAGE = 'Enter postcode'); wired at select_billing_address_validation.js:22-23 (STRING_EMPTY + ANY_REQUIRED → this message). postalCode is carried as a hidden input on the select-address form (selectBillingAddress.html:23), so this is a defensive/tamper path rather than a user-typed field — but the copy is verbatim in the legacy source.

validationMessages.trigger: Continue submitted with a hidden postalCode value that fails POSTCODE_REGEX (Joi STRING_PATTERN).
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification validation_messages.js:39 (UK_POSTCODE_ERROR_MESSAGE = 'Enter valid UK postcode'); wired at select_billing_address_validation.js:21 (STRING_PATTERN → this message). Same message constant as the find-address pattern error, reused on the select-address form's hidden postalCode.

validationMessages.trigger: Submitting 'Find address' with an empty or invalid postcode
validationMessages.resolvedBy: legacy — the six legacy-sourced messages above now supply this copy (previously a genuine gap because no admissible trace/QA source rendered it). Retained per the do-not-delete rule as the record of the original gap and its resolution.
validationMessages.evidence: Not observed in any trace — this is a zero-error happy-path trace (0 errors). No error state for the postcode field or address select was rendered in the mined trace, and page objects FindAnAddressPage.ts / BillingSelectTheAddressPage.ts expose NO error/validation-message locators. Copy has since been recovered from the authorised legacy source (ipaffs-frontend-notification validation_messages.js + the two Joi validators) — see the legacy entries above.

openQuestions: This spec covers TWO distinct URLs/pages in one logical step, matching the page name 'postcode lookup and select': (1) /billing-details/find-address (H1 'Find an address', postcode + Find address) and (2) /billing-details/select-address?postcode={pc} (H1 'Select the address', dropdown + Continue). The evidence pointers were correct: action 102 is on page 1, action 104 is on page 2. Downstream may want to split these into two page specs.

No validation/error copy captured — the mined trace is a clean happy path (0 errors). Postcode-required, invalid-postcode, no-addresses-found, and address-not-selected error messages are all GAPS; mine an error trace or the page objects (FindAnAddressPage.ts, BillingSelectTheAddressPage.ts) to fill them.

The postcode input carries no HTML 'required' attribute and the select carries no 'required' attribute — validation is server-side. Marked required=false to reflect the observed DOM, but both are functionally mandatory to progress.

The address <select> option[0] is a non-selectable count placeholder ('46 addresses found'); the real address options carry values 0..45. The address list is reference/lookup data from a postcode service — the new app should source it live, never hardcode. optionCount 47 and first ~15 options captured verbatim; remainder truncated (all same street for this test postcode B69 3NE).
The 'Enter address manually' and 'I cannot find the address in the list' links both route to a separate manual-entry address page (/billing-details/address) — a sibling page not mined here.

This whole billing-address flow is CUC-only (Common User Charge); reached only when the CUC billing journey is active. Corroborated: the QA notification-config field isCuc defaults to false (ched-d-workflows.ts:143) and gates the entire billing block (ched-d-workflows.ts:355 `if (isCuc)`), so both find-address and select-address are variant-only branches.

Source 2 corroboration adds NO new fields or controls: the page objects FindAnAddressPage.ts and BillingSelectTheAddressPage.ts drive only the postcode textbox, 'Find address' button, address combobox and 'Continue' button — a strict subset of what the trace already captured. The traces are the richer source here (they also captured the Details 'Help for non-UK businesses' block, the 'Enter address manually' / 'I cannot find the address in the list' / 'Cancel and return to notification' links and the 'Change' postcode link), none of which the tests exercise.

The manual-entry escape hatches ('Enter address manually', 'I cannot find the address in the list') are never exercised by any CHED-D test — the CUC workflow always searches a valid postcode and selects an address by index (ched-d-workflows.ts:357-358). Their target page (/billing-details/address) is unmined and remains a sibling-page gap.

Field required-ness is unchanged after corroboration: neither the postcode input nor the address select carries an HTML required attribute in the trace, and no test asserts a required-field rule. Both remain required=false to reflect observed reality while being functionally mandatory (server-side validation) — kept as an open question rather than upgraded.
